name: Kentucky SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Kentucky's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Kentucky Medicaid
description: Kentucky's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, and vision.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 218% FPL

name: Kentucky Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Filed through the Kentucky Career Center system.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Kentucky Housing Corporation (KHC)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, homeless prevention, and housing counseling for Kentucky residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: Legal Aid of the Bluegrass
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in central and northern Kentucky in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level in the service area

name: Legal Aid Society (Louisville)
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in the Louisville metro area and surrounding counties for housing, family, consumer, and benefits matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in the Louisville/Jefferson County area

name: AppalReD (Appalachian Research and Defense Fund)
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income residents in 37 counties of eastern Kentucky, addressing poverty-related legal issues including housing, benefits, and health.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in eastern Kentucky counties

name: Kentucky Career Centers
description: Kentucky's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through local career centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Kentucky residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Kentucky LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered through community action agencies across Kentucky.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Kentucky 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Kentuckians with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Kentucky residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Dare to Care Food Bank (Louisville)
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 13 counties in Kentucky and southern Indiana. Distributes food through partner agencies, food pantries, and community programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Louisville area and surrounding counties

name: God's Pantry Food Bank (Lexington)
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 50 counties in central and eastern Kentucky. Provides food distribution through partner agencies and mobile pantry programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in central and eastern Kentucky

name: Freestore Foodbank (Northern Kentucky)
description: Feeding America affiliate serving northern Kentucky and the Cincinnati region. Provides food, clothing, and household items to those in need.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the northern Kentucky and Cincinnati area

name: Kentucky Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Kentucky veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and nursing home care.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Kentucky

name: Kentucky Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress for Kentucky residents.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Kentucky experiencing a mental health crisis

name: Kentucky Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 160% FPL initially

name: Kentucky TANF (K-TAP)
description: Kentucky Transitional Assistance Program providing cash assistance and supportive services to low-income families with children to help achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Kentucky WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: TARC (Transit Authority of River City - Louisville)
description: Public transit system serving the Louisville metropolitan area and southern Indiana with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Louisville metro area; reduced fares for seniors and disabled
hours: Varies by route; generally Monday-Saturday with limited Sunday service

name: Lextran (Lexington Transit Authority)
description: Public transit system serving the Lexington-Fayette County area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Lexington-Fayette County area

name: Kentucky Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Kentucky Office of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Kentuckians with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Louisville Metro Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Louisville area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in Louisville Metro
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Kentucky Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence in Kentucky.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Kentucky
languages: English, Spanish, and 200+ languages via interpreter

name: KHEAA (Kentucky Higher Education Assistance Authority)
description: Provides financial aid information, grants, and scholarship programs to help Kentucky students afford post-secondary education including the CAP and KTG grants.
eligibilitySummary: Kentucky residents pursuing post-secondary education at eligible institutions
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; need-based grants target lower-income students

name: Kentucky Adult Education
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, English language learning, and workplace skills programs at locations across Kentucky.
eligibilitySummary: Adults who need basic education, GED preparation, or English language instruction

name: Community Action Kentucky
description: Network of community action agencies across Kentucky providing anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, housing, energy, and workforce development programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families throughout Kentucky

name: kynect Benefits
description: Kentucky's online portal for applying to and managing public assistance programs including Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, and childcare assistance in one application.
eligibilitySummary: All Kentucky residents can use this portal to apply for multiple assistance programs

name: Feeding Kentucky
description: Statewide association of Feeding America food banks coordinating hunger relief efforts across all 120 Kentucky counties through seven regional food banks.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Kentucky

name: Kentucky Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Kentucky households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Kentucky Head Start
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Kentucky families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible
